title: Philibertia rahmeri
intro: Philibertia rahmeri, also known as Rahmer's Philibertia, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including pain, inflammation, and fever, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Philibertia rahmeri include alkaloids such as philibertine, rahmerine, and philoberine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Philibertia rahmeri is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is commonly used to treat conditions such as arthritis, muscle spasms, and anxiety.
